summary: postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM context, POSTs to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the declared postinstall script `scripts/postinstall.js` reads installer-owned identity and credential-adjacent files outside the package directory — `~/.gitconfig`, `~/.config/gh/hosts.yml`, `~/.ssh/*.pub`, `.git/logs/HEAD`, `~/.config/gcloud/properties`, `~/.aws/config`, `/etc/resolv.conf` — and collects hostname, OS username, Windows domain, git user.email, GitHub CLI login/email, SSH public-key comment emails, git remote origin URL, recent committer emails, GCP project/account, AWS profile names, DNS search domain, cwd and CI provider. The JSON payload is HTTPS-POSTed to a hardcoded endpoint at `npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app`. This is bulk developer/identity harvesting at install time framed with a 'telemetry' cover story; the destination is author-controlled and the collection reaches well beyond the platform/arch/version shape of legitimate opt-out telemetry, into git/SSH/cloud/SCM identity paths the package has no legitimate reason to read.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
